title: U.S. Air Quality Trends Dashboard
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Multi-line time series chart showing six criteria pollutant emission trends alongside GDP growth from 1970-2025

educational.topic: U.S. Air Quality Trends and the Clean Air Act
learningObjectives: Assess the effectiveness of the Clean Air Act by analyzing emission trends for all six criteria pollutants alongside economic indicators
Interpret dual-axis time series charts comparing pollution and economic data
Identify key policy milestones and their impact on emission reduction rates
Evaluate the claim that environmental regulation harms economic growth
prerequisites: Understanding of air pollutants and their health effects

keyQuestions: Does the data support or refute the claim that environmental regulation harms the economy?
Which pollutant was most successfully controlled and what policy drove the change?
Why do different pollutants decline at different rates?
What role do recessions play in temporary pollution reductions?
commonMisconceptions: Environmental regulation always hurts the economy (GDP grew 330% while all pollutants declined)
Air quality has gotten worse over time (all six criteria pollutants have declined dramatically since 1970)
The Clean Air Act was a single policy (it was amended multiple times with different provisions targeting different pollutants)
assessmentOpportunities: Have students identify inflection points in the data and match them to policy milestones
Ask students to construct an evidence-based argument about regulation and economic growth
Challenge students to predict which pollutant will be hardest to reduce further and why
